item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1000s block was great cuz people could just post their fetish porn on here and have it get upvotes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might have been a good idea but I really don't like the hamfisted symbolism. It's like the whole article is constantly yelling out "DO YOU GET THE SUBTLE MESSAGE?! IT'S ABOUT HOW COSMETIC SURGERY TAKES OVER YOUR BEING AND MAKES YOU LOSE YOUR HUMANITY" which I always found to be a rather condescending moral to begin with whenever movies, TV shows or some other medium do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Breast implants….stamped with THE FACTORY… +1 even if the entire thing was redacted. Way too horrible an idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necro post. Had to +1 that comment though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those implants exist, I tell you hwat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mobile Task Force Beta-008 "Boob Grabbers"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The gradual conversion from organic tissue to silicon is bad enough, but the loss of natural healing is a personal fear of me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i didn't click on that for fear of my sanity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, it works better as a Factory item, but I've just seen too many SCPs that enforce a cliche. (And did the side effects really have to be loss of intelligence?)

I agree, except the Factory is very often malicious.

Sure, it does have side effects , but rather than intent, these very well might be caused by limitations of the technology used.

Bull. Of course it was intended. What Lireci is reacting to is likely exactly what these things were designed to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh. I just don't like it. Yes, it works better as a Factory item, but I've just seen too many SCPs that enforce a cliche. (And did the side effects really have to be loss of intelligence?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um, the point you are missing is that it's made on purpose, by the Factory as an anomalous consumer product, and, well, rather than Gone Horribly Right, it's using methods out of our reality to give some sort of people a product they'd be very interested in.
Sure, it does have side effects , but rather than intent, these very well might be caused by limitations of the technology used. Besides… things like regular implants, liposuction, and other cosmetic surgery techniques do have side effects that are also bad (and compared to the payoff of this one, perhaps more so) , yet people do it, and spend appalling amounts of cash on such. And don't get me even started about stuff like models eating tissue paper soaked in orange juice to combat hunger while trying not to gain weight….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of my pet-peeve cliches that's not on the Big List. Something that enforces the way people think the world is or should be.

SCP-1096 reifies the stereotype of the woman who fills herself with plastic because she has a rigid plastic personality, who has nothing going for her but her looks and so turns herself into a plastic doll.

The effects of SCP-1096 are broadly similar to what you should expect from undergoing plastic surgery inside SCP-1000-J. SCP-288 operates on a similar principle of trying to conform perfectly to a narrow-minded set of expectations and Gone Horribly Right.

Dunno if that clears anything up. It's more a visceral intuition than a well-reasoned theoretical framework of discourse.

ETA: Some of the other comments in this thread are illustrative, I think.

This SCP explains a lot…

Where the hell is a Factory product catalog, I need to make some… orders.

The first and last implant for the most image-conscious women imaginable…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, what? What does this have to do with either of those SCPs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP explains a lot…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels too much like something from SCP-1000-J, only intended unironically. While that *can* be done well, it's too late to actually build an SCP on the concept because SCP-288 beat you to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um, the implant on the picture actually has a serial number. Containment breach?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe a subsection of SCP-000-J?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here ya go!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where the hell is a Factory product catalog, I need to make some… orders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EW GROSS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here you go.

(SFW, I promise.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or Barbie dolls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like it could use a whole lot more titpics in the discussion…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see no reason why the effect should be any different for males. I could simply turn them into an idea male figure. It may not be designed for males, but since the effect is based on the subjects perception of the ideal figure,it ought to still work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually…

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buttock_augmentation]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Death Becomes Her"! I knew this was reminding me of something, but I couldn't remember what!

This is not a bad thing, as the movie was going for a comic effect, whereas this definitely isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You wish!

(I wish too…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you drain the fluid from the implants? Most implants nowadays are water-filled within a silicone skin, although there are still some with liquid silicone. What would happen if you injected said fluid into a person (say, the lips, cheeks, etc)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you should include a male D-class. It is something I would expect the Foundation to do, if nothing else, to see what would happen. Another option would be have one of the 15 sets be a set of pec implants as Drewbear mentions.

Maybe the documentation could make mention of "other products in our line" or something like that.

Reminds me of the movie "Death Becomes Her". Overall, excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ass implants?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are also such things as pec implants for men. Ab implants, too (for that perfect 6- or 8-pack, of course).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought about that, and it's something that could be expanded with. I would think either an excruciatingly painful change into a female figure (but that seems to edge in on 113's territory too much), or simply nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I do have one question: What happens if these are implanted in a male subject?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Created as a response to Bright's challenge to create a breast implant SCP.

The first and last implant for the most image-conscious women imaginable…
